translation_findings.en: D. Option D. Worker Dispatch Contract
translation_findings.issue: EN choice text starts with duplicated marker: 'D. Option D. Worker Dispatch Contract'. The 'D. ' prefix appears twice — once as the convention marker, once embedded as literal 'Option D.' text. This violates the en choice-marker convention (single 'A./B./C./D.' prefix) and is visible release-side. Note: choices[0..2].en are clean ('A. Contract for Work agreement', 'B. Employment Contract', 'C. Sales contract'); only choice D is malformed. This is the correct answer (answer_index=3), so the visible defect is on the answer line.
translation_findings.suggested_fix: Replace en with 'D. Worker Dispatch Contract' (drop the embedded 'Option D.' fragment).

translation_findings.jp: ア．生活の安定、労働力の質的向上のために最低の賃金を保障した法律
translation_findings.zh: A. 为稳定生活、提升劳动力素质而保障最低工资的法律
translation_findings.en: A. A law that guarantees minimum wages to stabilize livelihoods and improve the quality of the workforce
translation_findings.issue: Faithful translation — for IT Passport context, '最低の賃金を保障した法律' actually refers to the Minimum Wage Act (最低賃金法), a distractor against Labor Standards Act. EN rendering 'guarantees minimum wages' is semantically correct. No defect; logged because the JP wording is slightly unusual ('保障した' past-tense framing) and the translation handles it cleanly. PASS at leaf level.

narrative: 5 question entities, 4 choices each (20 choice leaves × jp/zh/en = 60) + 5 stems × jp/zh/en = 15, total 75 leaves. ONE release-impacting EN choice-marker defect: question 4 (X社/A社 派遣 context), correct-answer choice D, EN text has duplicated marker 'D. Option D. Worker Dispatch Contract'. zh + jp markers and all other choices/stems clean. zh choice markers correctly A./B./C./D., jp correctly ア./イ./ウ./エ. Semantic faithfulness across all questions is solid (no inversion, no answer drift, terminology like 不正アクセス助長行為 → 'facilitating unauthorized access' / '助长非法访问' is correct). FAIL verdict due to the en marker defect on the answer line of q4.
